common.reportedByRunnerHello: Reported by local service hello snapshots.

search.title: Search
search.hintProjects: Search project names, slugs, or descriptions.

user.noWorkspaces: No workspace has been created yet.

hero.title: Spark local cockpit
hero.lede: Workspace state, Spark runtime projections, and evidence appear here once a workspace directory comes online.

noWorkspaceHero.title: Create your first workspace
noWorkspaceHero.lede: Pick a profile, fill in the workspace details, then run the generated command on your machine.

workspaceHome.title: Workspaces
workspaceHome.lede: Pick a workspace to inspect its bound local directory, pending asks, projects, and artifacts.

emptyWorkspace.title: Profile and command
emptyWorkspace.body: Pick a profile, then run the generated command in your terminal.

steps.title: Pick a profile
steps.description: Select a workspace profile and fill in the required details.

steps.title: Run the generated command
steps.description: Copy the command and run it in your terminal. Pass the directory inline, or leave it off and answer the prompt.

steps.title: Open the workspace
steps.description: Once the target directory comes online, confirm the registration here and open the workspace.

stepActions.commandCreatedHint: Copy and run this in your terminal. Pass a directory after register, or omit it and answer the prompt.
stepActions.copyInsecureContext: Clipboard requires a secure browser context.
stepActions.copyUnavailable: Clipboard API is unavailable in this browser.
stepActions.waitForCommand: Confirm the profile details above. The registration command appears here as soon as it's ready.
stepActions.waitForWorkspace: Run the registration command first. This step unlocks once the target directory comes online.

form.name: Workspace name
form.freshProfileDescription: Start with an empty workspace profile.
form.gitProfileDescription: Import agents and resources from a GitHub profile repo.

runnerRequired.title: Register a workspace directory first
runnerRequired.body: Generate a registration command, then run it in your terminal. The final step unlocks once the workspace directory is online.

runners.title: Local registrations
runners.tokenCreatedHint: Run the generated command in your terminal. The workspace directory appears here after the first heartbeat.
runners.emptyBody: Generate a registration command, then run navia ws register on the target machine.
runners.noTokensBody: Create a one-time workspace registration token. The full command is shown once and cannot be recovered.

runnerEmpty.title: No local services registered yet
runnerEmpty.body: Waiting for a local service to register through

formMessages.profileInvalid: The workspace profile fields are invalid.
formMessages.commandCreated: Registration command created. It is shown once and cannot be recovered.
formMessages.registerWorkspaceFirst: Bring the target workspace directory online before opening the workspace.
formMessages.unsupportedProfileSource: This workspace profile source is not supported.
formMessages.loadProfileFailed: Could not load the workspace profile.
formMessages.workspaceRequired: Enter a workspace name and slug.

hero.title: Workspace projects
hero.lede: Projects group cockpit routing with Spark task graph, ask, invocation, and artifact projections from the active workspace.

emptyWorkspace.title: Create a workspace first
emptyWorkspace.body: Projects belong to a workspace. Register a local workspace directory, then create the workspace from Home.

list.title: Projects
list.emptyBody: Create the first project to collect inbox requests, task graph snapshots, and evidence.

formMessages.nameSlugRequired: Enter a project name and slug.

graph.title: Task graph
graph.body: Navia renders the latest Spark-owned execution snapshot from its projection cache. Statuses, dependencies, and invocation links remain owned by Spark runtime state.

command.offlinePending: offline commands stay pending until the local service reconnects to the Spark bridge
command.title: Start a task
command.promptPlaceholder: Describe what should run inside this workspace.
command.empty: No task start command queued for this project yet.

delivery.pendingOffline: Pending — waiting for local service to reconnect
delivery.sent: Delivered — waiting for ack or reject

tasks.title: Task graph
tasks.empty: No task graph snapshot ingested for this project yet.
tasks.noUpstream: No upstream blockers in this snapshot.

formMessages.taskRequired: Enter a task title and prompt.
formMessages.queued: Task queued for the owning local service.

hero.title: Inbox
hero.lede: Asks and reviews from Spark-backed runs wait here until an operator answers. Nothing times out automatically.

emptyWorkspace.title: Create a workspace first
emptyWorkspace.body: Inbox items belong to the workspace reported by a local workspace binding.

empty.title: No human requests yet
empty.body: When a Spark-backed run calls ask_user, review, approval, or blocker, the request shows up here.

response.title: Answer request
response.previewOnly: Preview-only context. No answer is required for this prompt.
response.operatorNotePlaceholder: Optional delivery note recorded with the audit trail
response.closedBody: This inbox item is no longer pending.

formMessages.alreadyResolved: This inbox item has already been resolved.
formMessages.unsupportedStatus: This response status is not supported.

hero.title: Repos & resources
hero.lede: Register workspace-level resources that agents and projects can reference. Repos are first-class; docs, URLs, files, tools, and secret references sit alongside them.

emptyWorkspace.title: Create a workspace first
emptyWorkspace.body: Resources are scoped to a server-visible workspace.

list.title: Resources
list.emptyBody: Add a repository, doc, URL, file, tool, or secret reference for future projects.

create.title: Add resource
create.name: Name
create.notesPlaceholder: What should agents know about this resource?

formMessages.unsupportedKind: This resource kind is not supported.

hero.title: Agents
hero.lede: Reusable workspace-level agent specs. Spark-backed execution uses these specs to describe preferred roles, instructions, and enabled state.

emptyWorkspace.title: Create a workspace first
emptyWorkspace.body: Agent specs are scoped to the active workspace.

list.title: Agent specs
list.emptyBody: Add a reusable worker, reviewer, planner, or imported spec for the workspace.

create.title: Add agent spec
create.name: Name
create.descriptionPlaceholder: Reviews project evidence and task results
create.instructionsPlaceholder: What should this agent focus on?

formMessages.unsupportedSource: This agent source is not supported.
formMessages.createFailed: Could not create the agent spec.

hero.title: Artifacts
hero.lede: Evidence produced by Spark-backed runs and operators surfaces here as projection metadata. Canonical content stays in Spark/local artifact stores until you request a preview or export.

emptyWorkspace.title: Create a workspace first
emptyWorkspace.body: Artifact projections are scoped to a locally owned workspace.

empty.title: No artifacts yet
empty.body: Local execution artifacts and operator-response artifacts show up here with provenance and content pointers.

cache.title: Preview and export pointer
cache.body: Navia keeps preview and export files in the server artifact cache. Canonical content stays local and is not copied eagerly.

preview.title: Cached content
preview.nonTextPrefix: Preview body is not text; download via

statusHints.missing: The server has not fetched preview content yet. Use Prepare preview cache to fetch one when the runtime offers it.
statusHints.fetching: The server is fetching the preview cache.
statusHints.too_large: The artifact body is larger than the inline preview budget. Open the canonical content via the runtime path.
statusHints.unsupported_binary: Binary artifacts are not previewed inline. Use the canonical pointer to download them.
statusHints.error: The server could not build the preview. Check the cache record for details.
statusHints.evicted: The cached preview was evicted. Re-prepare to fetch a fresh one.

formMessages.prepareFailed: Could not prepare the artifact preview.

general.title: Settings
general.lede: Manage the current workspace, registration commands, and local connection health from a single console.

account.title: Local owner
account.body: Owner profile, session preferences, and team access controls will live here.

workspace.title: Workspace preferences
workspace.body: Default profiles, naming rules, and project templates will move into this section.

profiles.title: Workspace profiles
profiles.body: Profile import, save-as-profile, and Git sync controls will be managed here.

hero.title: Workspace settings
hero.lede: Manage the current workspace and its local connection.
hero.ledeRest: Workspace and project navigation continue to live in the main app.

workspace.title: Workspace settings
workspace.body: Edit the server-visible name, route, and description for this workspace.
workspace.name: Workspace name

enrollment.title: Registration tokens
enrollment.body: Mint one-time workspace registration tokens. The plaintext token is shown only at creation time; afterwards Settings lists metadata only.
enrollment.label: Token label
enrollment.tokenCreatedHint: Copy the command now. Navia keeps only the token hash and cannot show this secret again. The workspace directory appears here once the command connects.
enrollment.emptyBody: Generate a registration command to bring a local workspace directory online.

metrics.offlineHint: Stale-connection sweep is not enabled yet

runner.title: Registered local services
runner.emptyBody: Generate a registration command from this page, then run navia ws register against

steps.title: Create a workspace registration token
steps.description: Mint a one-time workspace registration token from the local owner session and exchange it for local service credentials.

steps.title: Start the Spark daemon
steps.description: The local service owns the server WebSocket and workspace registry, then invokes Spark runtime primitives for task, artifact, and ask/review projections.

steps.title: Connect thin agent plugins
steps.description: Pi, Codex, Claude Code, and future adapters talk to the local service over local IPC; they do not connect to Navia directly.

bindings.title: Reported directories
bindings.empty: No workspace directories reported yet. After
bindings.emptyRest: directories show up here first, then graduate into the main workspace navigation.

formMessages.workspaceRequired: Enter a workspace name and path identifier.
formMessages.slugUsed: That workspace path identifier is already in use.
formMessages.commandCreated: Registration command created. It is shown once and cannot be viewed again.
formMessages.tokenNotActive: That registration token was not active.

setup.title: Bring your local workspaces into a single precise cockpit.
setup.lede: The browser only talks to the local Navia server. Spark runtime state owns execution, artifacts, and task graphs; Navia renders projections.
setup.panelCopy: This local-first v0.1 server needs one owner before it can manage workspace connections.

errors.displayNameMin: Display name must be at least 2 characters.
errors.ownerExists: The Navia owner is already set up.
